meaning: adj.全部的,所有的,整个的adv.完全,更加,越发 pron.全部
examples: I do all the work and he gets all the glory.
We did all the work and she gets all the credit!
They're all good players, but she's the best of all.
So for all the talk, all the money, the troops have had little to do.
We rank all the hitters in baseball and say who earned his keep in 2000.
